title: agent-core: loop strategy gaps - concurrent, group-chat, handoff
summary: Issue #589 tracks agent-core strategy hardening gaps: concurrent timeout and partial results, group-chat moderator validation, handoff context and target validation, external loop cancellation, retryable oversight, enforced delegation timeoutMs, and strategy composition.
issueFindings: ConcurrentLoopRunner currently batches agents and awaits Promise.allSettled without per-agent timeout controls.
GroupChatLoopRunner currently asks a moderator for output and selects the first configured id found by string inclusion.
HandoffLoopRunner currently accepts a handoffTarget from output without validating it against configured agent ids.
HandoffLoopRunner switches active agent ids but does not build or transfer structured handoff context.
AgentLoop.run() exposes no external cancellation token or signal.
SubagentInvokerImpl.delegate() uses OversightRunner.review(output, 0), making oversight single-pass.
OversightConfig declares timeoutMs, but SubagentInvokerImpl does not enforce it directly.
AgentLoopStrategy is a flat discriminator union, so composition/mixing needs an explicit additive contract.

methodologyChoices: Runtime-read the GitHub issue and current code evidence to prevent spec drift.
Trace brownfield runtime call paths before editing implementation files.
Author failing Vitest contract tests before implementation.
Use shell tasks with expectedExitCode for deterministic red, targeted, and full verification gates.
Use agent review only for semantic/API compatibility evaluation.

acceptanceFocus: Concurrent strategy exposes and enforces per-agent timeout semantics without discarding completed agents' partial results.
Concurrent strategy reports timed-out and rejected agents predictably while preserving deterministic agent id ordering.
AgentLoop can be cancelled from outside run()/iteration execution and transitions to a clear terminal state without orphaning callbacks or in-flight timeout state.
Group-chat moderator selection uses a structured or otherwise unambiguous contract and validates selected speakers against configured agent ids.
Invalid or ambiguous group-chat moderator selections fail clearly instead of silently falling back to an arbitrary speaker.
Handoff strategy validates handoff targets against configured agent ids before switching control.
Handoff strategy transfers structured context to the next agent according to a typed contract.
Subagent delegation enforces oversight.timeoutMs or a renamed equivalent timeout contract in SubagentInvokerImpl.
Delegation oversight supports configurable retry behavior that can produce revised output, not only repeat review of the same output.
Strategy composition/mixing has an explicit typed contract and tests, or the final review documents a maintainer-approved narrower contract.
Public exports and README documentation match the implemented contract.
docs/agent-layer-gaps.md is updated so fixed issue #589 gaps are not still listed as current gaps.
nonGoals: Do not implement broader agent-platform concurrent effect scheduling from #579 unless an agent-core public contract requires a small integration note.
Do not replace the external subagent/task routing architecture from #581.
Do not redesign all AgentLoop strategies beyond additive hardening needed for the issue.
Do not change unrelated agent-core session, tool, synthesis, or context-manager behavior.

plannedPhases.name: Runtime issue/spec read
plannedPhases.purpose: Read issue #589 title, body, labels, and comments at execution time.

plannedPhases.name: Runtime code evidence read
plannedPhases.purpose: Capture current loop, strategy, subagent, docs, and test evidence before authoring prompts that depend on the spec.

plannedPhases.name: Reuse audit
plannedPhases.purpose: Find existing timeout, cancellation, parallel dispatch, handoff, oversight, and process patterns to reuse.

plannedPhases.name: Architecture trace
plannedPhases.purpose: Record runtimeCallPaths and a compatible typed contract for all issue gaps.

plannedPhases.name: Contract tests and red gate
plannedPhases.purpose: Write failing Vitest tests from the runtime-read spec before implementation and require a red exit code.

plannedPhases.name: Implementation loop
plannedPhases.purpose: Iterate up to three times on the smallest compatible TypeScript implementation.

plannedPhases.name: Targeted verification
plannedPhases.purpose: Run agent-core loop/subagent tests, agent-core build, and git diff hygiene after each implementation attempt.

plannedPhases.name: Semantic contract review
plannedPhases.purpose: Compare the spec and diff directly for missing tests, unsafe fallbacks, timeout/cancellation leaks, and compatibility regressions.

plannedPhases.name: Full verification
plannedPhases.purpose: Run the agent-core package test suite, build, metadata verification, and diff hygiene.

plannedPhases.name: Final acceptance
plannedPhases.purpose: Confirm all issue criteria, docs, changed files, residual risks, and PR readiness.

qualityGates: Red gate: new targeted Vitest tests must fail before implementation with expectedExitCode 1.
Targeted loop/subagent tests pass after implementation.
Agent semantic review approves the diff against the runtime-read spec.
Final acceptance review confirms docs and docs/agent-layer-gaps.md match the implemented contract.
verificationCommands: cd packages/agent-core && npm exec --yes --package=vitest -- vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/loop/__tests__/agent-loop.test.ts src/subagent/__tests__/invoker.test.ts

breakpoints.when: Only if strategy composition or cancellation requires a non-additive public API contract.
Only if final acceptance identifies an unresolved public API decision or accepted residual risk.
